Hey guys, welcome back to the final lesson of module two. So we've done quite a bit of groundwork up to now. We've learned the coaching, the conversion process, we've gotten an overview of the journey between point A and point buyer. And we even got super intimate and close with our customer and hopefully dug up some really amazing insights in those exercises that we just did together. And in module three, we're going to really start putting it all together and executing the copy and the messaging that activates these things we just spoke about. But as a final, final step before we do that, I want us to ask a really simple question. I want us to ask, what is a buyer? Yes, it's true that a buyer is someone who pulls their credit card out and hits an order form and clicks buy. But what is really happening internally? You know, what would happen if we put a buyer on an operating table and started dissecting it to see the actual ingredients and makeup? And that's really the goal of this quick lesson. It's to see some of those invisible triggers at play. What's shifted? What is the substratum of buying activity? You know, what are these parts that make a hole? You know, in short, we're going to be starting with the end in mind and then reverse engineering those pieces to make sure that by the end of our funnel, we've literally constructed a buyer. Cool. So what are these ingredients that make up a buyer? You know, a buyer is someone who has an active interest in solving the problem or achieving the outcome your product delivers. And that's kind of basic, yet it's our job to get them more intimately acquainted with that desire as well as their newfound ability to solve it. You know, but if it's not already there, at least as a seed, if it's not dormant, then we won't have much luck. However, beyond the outcome our product solves, our buyer is also someone who's been given the opportunity to leverage and express their unique gifts and circumstances. You know, I call this cashing in on their exclusivity points. Now, this comes back to asking the question we did earlier and what ways is my prospect exclusively empowered to use and benefit from my product or to even embark on this journey? And it's a super powerful buyer's trait that we want to take full advantage of. Because when we do this and do this well, our audience feels, our prospect feels like the product was created just for them and their unique makeup and situation. They get to leverage these things. They're proud of leverage their current situation, even leverage past failures in order to get the result. And this is a super powerful trigger that makes up a buyer. So you'll want to be super specific in your qualifiers and you'll want to raise the bar for your best customers to have the satisfaction of vaulting over it. So you'll see a little later the perfect for you template in the closing subsequence or actually the sale sequence will be one of the main tools that you use to leverage this trigger. But moving on, a buyer is also someone who is intimately aware of the cost of not taking action. You know, we talk a lot about risk reversal. You're going to learn something a little later called risk revealer, which talks all about getting them acquainted with exactly what happens if they don't even make a decision right now. They allow themselves to continue going in as status quo. You know, you don't need to fully mitigate the risk if you can show that the bigger risk actually comes within action. A buyer also has renewed belief and optimism that the outcome can be achieved and more specifically that they can do it. You know, and this all starts with what we spoke about earlier, which is grounding those aspirations in a complete understanding and validation of their current reality. So, if you ground those lofty outcomes, it becomes very hard to believe that this is achievable and this is achievable for them. So that's where confidence and trust and just validating and reflecting back their current reality really, really goes a long way. But a buyer is also someone who can enjoy the payoff of vindicating past mistakes or reframing them with new meaning. There was a movie that came out a few years ago. It wasn't a very popular one, but it was called Someday This Pain Will Be Useful. You know, and that's a very healing and powerful human drive is to just be able to reframe past failures, past things that we weren't so proud of, you know, and that becomes included in the vindication. So for someone who can enjoy the payoff of vindicating past failures is a very powerful trigger, you know, and a very powerful part of the makeup of a successful person. Buyer, but a buyer is also someone, someone who feels in control and empowered. You know, we resist actions that we feel are being forced upon us. We resist and get into self-protection mode whenever we feel like we're being backed into a corner and not given a choice. So a buyer on the contrary is someone who feels like they're asserting their own will on the situation that they're acting within their own volition, that they're not being manipulated or forced or pushed into a certain decision, but that they're actually getting to co-created and make it themselves. So in module seven, you're going to see something I call the build your own offer close, you know, which really activates some of these triggers of, you know, co-creation of participation, of feeling like they have enacted their own power in making that decision. You know, so a big part in empowering people to assert their will and commit also involves lowering the bar. I know we just spoke about raising the bar. We raise the bar as far as the things that, you know, aspects of their past, you know, qualifications they have in order to clear it and be perfect for our product. But we also lower the bar of the commitment because the commitment is the scariest part, right? So minimum viable commitment. It's a concept we're going to go deeper into later, but this is a big, big part of building a buyer. You know, if we want people to become customers, we need to lower the bar of what that means. Now, if we can redefine a buyer as someone who doesn't necessarily have unshakable iron clad confidence, but just enough to tip the scale, tip the scales and give it a try, we give our prospects not just a much more attainable, but a more realistic image of a buyer to relate with. You know, it's the difference between saying, let's go on a date next week and see where it goes versus let's spend the next 70 years, grow old and die together. You know, one of them is this enormous commitment that requires this iron clad confidence that just isn't realistic, isn't natural to anybody. So we lower the commitment bar. We redefine a buyer is not someone who has this crazy, unshakable, focused iron clad confidence that it's going to work and that they're going to be able to do it, but just enough, just enough to give it a try to show up, to play full and see where that takes them. So lowering the bar as far as a minimum viable commitment, you know, and we're going to go a lot deeper into this, deeper into this topic in module seven and how to apply it. But for now, before we get there, I just want you to briefly consider, you know, what lowering the bar as a buyer might mean for you. How can you make it more passable? How can you make your prospect feel like there's skepticism? Their skepticism is well-convened included and actually part of what being a buyer is. And then finally, the final ingredient is something we've all heard before, but it's worth repeating. A buyer is someone who is giving you hard earned money, you know, and to do that, they must trust you. They must trust you to get them results. They must trust you to deliver what you promised. And they must trust you, you know, to continue caring about them after a payment has cleared. And this is quite a vast topic, you know, and this goes beyond testimonials and case studies. That's one level of trust. But another level of trust that we're really honing in on in this course is, you know, a powerful way to build trust, being to mirror back their reality accurately, specifically, you know, that's how we leverage specificity to the highest degree. Specificity when used and leveraged is an ingredient for trust. You know, it's a trust that we get them, we see them, we know exactly what they're experiencing. And therefore there is that trust in our diagnosis and in our prescription for that problem. So there's going to be a whole other lesson on this later. We won't go too deep into it right now, but I just want to expand that definition of what trust is. And on that note, we are done module two. And before we get to module three, you know, I'm just going to remind you that from here on out, we're going to be going step by step in really writing out your funnel or your launch campaign. And we're going to start with the segmentation and confirmation sequences in module in module three. Now a prerequisite to move forward is, you know, that you've nailed down which launch methodology you plan to go with. So, you know, we're going to reference some excellent ones in the resources section. These are people that we've vetted, that we've communicated with, you know, and just really have solid proven launch methodologies. So you could choose one from the resource section if you haven't done so already, or you could actually dive into our bonus. If you're really just starting out and you want to do this as quickly as possible, you know, with as little layers and pieces and, you know, moving parts as possible, I fully encourage you to take a look at the minimum viable launch funnel in our bonus section, which is going to show you how to do this with email only using nothing but your email software. And then of course, you always have the option to, if you are experienced in the field of building your funnels and your launch strategies, you have every right, you know, to just go with what you want to personally use and just build it yourself, knowing that these five sub sequences that you're going to be learning from here on out are totally funnel agnostic. So they should be able to plug in perfectly with what you're doing. So that's it. You have those options. Choose one now and whatever you are confident in what you are going to be launching with. I invite you to join us in module three, where we will take a look at the segmentation and confirmation phase. I'll see you there.
